_comment: Issue #679 PR0-C — frozen characterization golden for the runtime exit
arbitration kernel (`runtime_status_transition_allowed`, db/sqlite.rs).
Full (from_status x to_status) -> allow/deny table, 7x7 = 49 cells,
14 allow / 35 deny. Generated from the implementation, then hand-audited
cell by cell and written down as data. Asserted black-box through the
real sqlite write paths (`runtime_set_status_tx` / `runtime_complete_tx`)
by tests/runtime_status_matrix_golden.rs.
Shape notes (the semantics this file pins):
* `starting` is the only state that may reach every non-terminal state
AND both exit terminals directly (spawn may settle anywhere).
* `running` / `idle` / `turn_pending` may exit (failed/exited);
running<->idle cycles; turn_pending resolves only via running.
* running->turn_pending and idle->turn_pending are DENIED: turn_pending
is an insert-time-only status today, never reached by transition.
* No self-loop is allowed (the diagonal is all deny).
* `superseded` is unreachable via status transition from ANY state:
rows only become superseded through `runtime_supersede_tx`'s guarded
UPDATE (old active row -> superseded + replacement insert, one tx).
* Terminal absorption: `failed` / `exited` / `superseded` rows accept
nothing — first terminal writer wins, forever.
Do NOT regenerate this file mechanically from the implementation; that
would defeat the golden. Any diff here is a semantic change to exit
arbitration and needs explicit review under #679.
